title: pandanus tectorius var. parksii
intro: Pandanus tectorius var. parksii, also known as the Tahitian pandanus or Park's pandanus, is a variety of the pandanus plant that is native to the Pacific Islands, specifically in the islands of Polynesia and Micronesia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Polynesian medicine, where it is used to treat a variety of ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pandanus tectorius var. parksii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in wounds. The plant is often prepared in herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and poultices, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
